  "markdown": "RLinf Documentation — RLinf documentation\n\nChoose version\n\n🤖 Ask AI\n\n# RLinf Documentation#\n\n# Welcome to RLinf!\n\nRLinf is a flexible and scalable open-source infrastructure designed for post-training foundation models via reinforcement learning. The ‘inf’ in RLinf stands for&#96;Infrastructure&#96;, highlighting its role as a robust backbone for next-generation training. It also stands for&#96;Infinite&#96;, symbolizing the system’s support for open-ended learning, continuous generalization, and limitless possibilities in intelligence development.\n\n---\n\n---\n\nRLinf is unique with:\n\nMacro-to-Micro Flow: a new paradigm M2Flow, which executes macro-level logical flows through micro-level execution flows, decoupling logical workflow construction (programmable) from physical communication and scheduling (efficiency).\n\nFlexible Execution Modes\n\nCollocated mode: shares all GPUs across all workers.\n\nDisaggregated mode: enables fine-grained pipelining.\n\nHybrid mode: a customizable combination of different placement modes, integrating both collocated and disaggregated modes.\n\nAuto Scheduling\n\nDynamic Scheduling: dynamically schedule resource allocation, maximizing resource utilization.\n\nStatic Scheduling: automatically select the most suitable execution mode based on the training workload, without the need for manual resource allocation.\n\nEmbodied Agent Support\n\nFast adaptation support for mainstream VLA models: [OpenVLA](https://github.com/openvla/openvla), [OpenVLA-OFT](https://github.com/moojink/openvla-oft), [π₀](https://github.com/Physical-Intelligence/openpi), [GR00T-N1.5](https://github.com/NVIDIA/Isaac-GR00T.git)\n\nSupport for mainstream CPU &amp; GPU-based simulators via standardized RL interfaces: [ManiSkill3](https://github.com/haosulab/ManiSkill), [LIBERO](https://github.com/Lifelong-Robot-Learning/LIBERO), [IsaacLab](https://github.com/isaac-sim/IsaacLab)\n\nEnabling the first RL fine-tuning of the π₀ model family with a flow-matching action expert.\n\nRLinf is fast with:\n\nHybrid mode with fine-grained pipelining: achieves a 120%+ throughput improvement compared to other frameworks.\n\nAutomatic Online Scaling Strategy: dynamically scales training resources, with GPU switching completed within seconds, further improving efficiency by 20–40% while preserving the on-policy nature of RL algorithms.\n\nRLinf is flexible and easy to use with:\n\nMultiple Backend Integrations\n\nFSDP + [RLinf Documentation — RLinf  documentation](https://rlinf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)<br/><br/># RLinf: Reinforcement Learning Infrastructure<br/><br/>RLinf is a flexible and scalable open-source RL infrastructure for post-training foundation models, published as arXiv:2509.15965.[1] It is designed to support reasoning RL (e.g., math with GRPO), embodied RL (e.g., vision-language agents in simulators), and agentic AI.[1]<br/><br/>## Core Innovation<br/><br/>RLinf is built on the **macro-to-micro flow transformation (M2Flow)** paradigm, which decouples logical workflow programming from execution planning.[1] This enables the system to automatically optimize how high-level RL workflows are executed, combining temporal and spatial decomposition with recomposition into optimized execution flows.[4]<br/><br/>## Performance<br/><br/>RLinf demonstrates significant speedups over state-of-the-art systems like veRL and Slime:[1]<br/>- 1.07×–1.70× speedup on math-reasoning RL with GRPO<br/>- Up to 2.43× speedup on embodied RL tasks<br/>- Evaluated across multiple model sizes (Qwen2.5, Qwen3-MoE, Open-VLA, OpenVLA-OFT) and algorithms (GRPO, PPO)<br/><br/>## Key Features<br/><br/>RLinf includes:[1][5]<br/>- Multiple execution modes (collocated, disaggregated, hybrid)<br/>- Elastic pipelining and context switching<br/>- Profiling-guided scheduling for optimal resource allocation<br/>- Support for mainstream VLA models and simulators (ManiSkill3, LIBERO, IsaacLab)<br/>- Multiple backend integrations (FSDP + Hugging Face, Megatron + SGLang)<br/>- Built-in RL algorithms (PPO, GRPO, DAPO, Reinforce++)<br/><br/>The open-source code is available on GitHub.[3] |

intelligence, and embodied intelligence. However, the inherent heterogeneity and dynamicity of RL workflows often lead to low hardware utilization and slow training on existing systems. In this paper, we present RLinf, a high-performance RL training system based on our key observation that the major roadblock to efficient RL training lies in system flexibility. To maximize flexibility and efficiency, RLinf is built atop a novel RL system design paradigm called macro-to-micro flow transformation (M2Flow), which automatically breaks down high-level, easy-to-compose RL workflows at both the temporal and spatial dimensions, and recomposes them into optimized execution flows. Supported by RLinf worker's adaptive communication capability, we devise context switching and elastic pipelining to realize M2Flow transformation, and a profiling-guided scheduling policy to generate optimal execution plans. Extensive evaluations on both reasoning RL and embodied RL tasks demonstrate that RLinf consistently outperforms state-of-the-art systems, achieving $1.07\\times-2.43\\times$ speedup in end-to-end training throughput.\n\n[https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2509.15965](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2509.15965)\n\narXiv-issued DOI via DataCite\n\n| Comments: |\n| --- |\n| Subjects: | Machine Learning (cs.LG); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Distributed, Parallel, and Cluster Computing (cs.DC) |\n| Cite as: | [arXiv:2509.15965](https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.15965) [cs.LG] |\n| (or   "markdown": "[2510.06710] RLinf-VLA: A Unified and Efficient Framework for Reinforcement Learning of Vision-Language-Action Models\n\n--\n\n# Computer Science Robotics\n\narXiv:2510.06710 (cs)\n\n[Submitted on 8 Oct 2025 ([v1](https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.06710v1)), last revised 7 Feb 2026 (this version, v2)]\n\n# Title:RLinf-VLA: A Unified and Efficient Framework for Reinforcement Learning of Vision-Language-Action Models\n\nAuthors: [Hongzhi Zang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Zang,+H), [Mingjie Wei](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Wei,+M), [Si Xu](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Xu,+S), [Yongji Wu](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Wu,+Y), [Zhen Guo](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Guo,+Z), [Yuanqing Wang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Wang,+P), [Liangzhi Shi](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Shi,+L), [Yuqing Xie](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Xie,+Y), [Zhexuan Xu](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Xu,+Z), [Zhihao Liu](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Liu,+Z), [Kang Chen](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Chen,+K), [Wenhao Tang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Tang,+W), [Quanlu Zhang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Zhang,+Q), [Weinan Zhang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Zhang,+W), [Chao Yu](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Yu,+C), [Yu Wang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Wang,+Y)\n\n[View PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.06710) [HTML (experimental)](https://arxiv.org/html/2510.06710v2)\n\n&gt; Abstract:Recent advances in vision-language-action (VLA) models have motivated the extension of their capabilities to embodied settings, where reinforcement learning (RL) offers a principled way to optimize task success through interaction. However, existing methods remain fragmented, lacking both a unified platform for fair comparison across architectures and algorithms and an efficient system design for scalable training. To address these challenges, we introduce RLinf-VLA, a unified and efficient framework for scalable RL training of VLA models. RLinf-VLA achieves unification by providing a unified interface that standardizes the integration of diverse VLA architectures, multiple RL algorithms, and heterogeneous simulators, enabling extensibility. To ensure efficiency, the system adopts a flexible resource allocation architecture for rendering, inference, and training workloads in RL pipelines. In particular, for GPU-parallelized simulators, RLinf-VLA introduces a hybrid fine-grained pipeline allocation strategy, yielding a 1.61x-1.88x training speedup. Using this unified system, models trained with RLinf-VLA demonstrate consistent performance improvements of approximately 20-85% across multiple simulation benchmarks, including LIBERO, ManiSkill, and RoboTwin. Furthermore, we distill a set of training practices for effective RL-based VLA training. We position RLinf-VLA as a foundational system to enable efficient, unified, and reproducible research in embodied intelligence.\n\n[https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2510.06710](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2510.06710)\n\narXiv-issued DOI via DataCite\n\n| Comments: |\n| --- |\n| Subjects: | Robotics (cs.RO) |\n| {
  "url": "https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.25889",
  "markdown": "[2510.25889] $π_\\texttt{RL}$: Online RL Fine-tuning for Flow-based Vision-Language-Action Models\n\n--\n\n# Computer Science Machine Learning\n\narXiv:2510.25889 (cs)\n\n[Submitted on 29 Oct 2025 ([v1](https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.25889v1)), last revised 29 Jan 2026 (this version, v3)]\n\n# Title:$π_\\texttt{RL}$: Online RL Fine-tuning for Flow-based Vision-Language-Action Models\n\nAuthors: [Kang Chen](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Chen,+K), [Zhihao Liu](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Liu,+Z), [Tonghe Zhang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Zhang,+T), [Zhen Guo](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Guo,+Z), [Si Xu](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Xu,+S), [Hao Lin](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Lin,+H), [Hongzhi Zang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Zang,+H), [Xiang Li](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Li,+X), [Quanlu Zhang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Zhang,+Q), [Zhaofei Yu](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Yu,+Z), [Guoliang Fan](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Fan,+G), [Tiejun Huang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Huang,+T), [Yu Wang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Wang,+Y), [Chao Yu](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Yu,+C)\n\n[View PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.25889) [HTML (experimental)](https://arxiv.org/html/2510.25889v3)\n\n&gt; Abstract:Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models enable robots to understand and perform complex tasks from multimodal input. Although recent work explores using reinforcement learning (RL) to automate the laborious data collection process in scaling supervised fine-tuning (SFT), applying RL to large-scale flow-based VLAs (\\eg, $\\pi_0$, $\\pi_{0.5}$) remains challenging due to intractable action log-likelihoods raised from flow matching. We address this challenge with $\\pi_{\\texttt{RL}}$, featuring two technical approaches: (1) \\textbf{Flow-Noise} models the denoising process as a discrete-time MDP with a learnable noise network for exact log-likelihood computation. (2) \\textbf{Flow-SDE} integrates denoising with agent-environment interaction, formulating a two-layer MDP that employs ODE-to-SDE conversion for efficient RL exploration. We evaluate $\\pi_{\\texttt{RL}}$ across various benchmarks, with experiments demonstrating that RL yields significant performance improvements in both in-distribution and out-of-distribution settings.\n\n[https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2510.25889](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2510.25889)\n\narXiv-issued DOI via DataCite\n\n| Subjects: | Machine Learning (cs.LG) |\n| --- | --- |\n| Cite   "markdown": "[2602.07837] RLinf-USER: A Unified and Extensible System for Real-World Online Policy Learning in Embodied AI\n\n--\n\n# Computer Science Robotics\n\narXiv:2602.07837 (cs)\n\n[Submitted on 8 Feb 2026 ([v1](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.07837v1)), last revised 12 Feb 2026 (this version, v3)]\n\n# Title:RLinf-USER: A Unified and Extensible System for Real-World Online Policy Learning in Embodied AI\n\nAuthors: [Hongzhi Zang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Zang,+H), [Shu'ang Yu](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Yu,+S), [Hao Lin](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Lin,+H), [Tianxing Zhou](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Zhou,+T), [Zefang Huang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Huang,+Z), [Zhen Guo](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Guo,+Z), [Xin Xu](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Xu,+X), [Jiakai Zhou](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Zhou,+J), [Yuze Sheng](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Sheng,+Y), [Shizhe Zhang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Zhang,+S), [Feng Gao](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Gao,+F), [Wenhao Tang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Tang,+W), [Yufeng Yue](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Yue,+Y), [Quanlu Zhang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Zhang,+Q), [Xinlei Chen](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Chen,+X), [Chao Yu](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Yu,+C), [Yu Wang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Wang,+Y)\n\n[View PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.07837) [HTML (experimental)](https://arxiv.org/html/2602.07837v3)\n\n&gt; Abstract:Online policy learning directly in the physical world is a promising yet challenging direction for embodied intelligence. Unlike simulation, real-world systems cannot be arbitrarily accelerated, cheaply reset, or massively replicated, which makes scalable data collection, heterogeneous deployment, and long-horizon effective training difficult. These challenges suggest that real-world policy learning is not only an algorithmic issue but fundamentally a systems problem. We present USER, a Unified and extensible SystEm for Real-world online policy learning. USER treats physical robots as first-class hardware resources alongside GPUs through a unified hardware abstraction layer, enabling automatic discovery, management, and scheduling of heterogeneous robots. To address cloud-edge communication, USER introduces an adaptive communication plane with tunneling-based networking, distributed data channels for traffic localization, and streaming-multiprocessor-aware weight synchronization to regulate GPU-side overhead. On top of this infrastructure, USER organizes learning as a fully asynchronous framework with a persistent, cache-aware buffer, enabling efficient long-horizon experiments with robust crash recovery and reuse of historical data. In addition, USER provides extensible abstractions for rewards, algorithms, and policies, supporting online imitation or reinforcement learning of CNN/MLP, generative policies, and large vision-language-action (VLA) models within a unified pipeline. Results in both simulation and the real world show that USER enables multi-robot coordination, heterogeneous manipulators, edge-cloud collaboration with large models, and long-running asynchronous training, offering a unified and extensible systems foundation for real-world online policy learning.\n\n[https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2602.07837](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2602.07837)\n\narXiv-issued DOI via DataCite\n\n| Subjects: | Robotics (cs.RO) |\n| --- | --- |\n| Cite as: | {
  "url": "https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.13977",
  "markdown": "[2602.13977] WoVR: World Models as Reliable Simulators for Post-Training VLA Policies with RL\n\n--\n\n# Computer Science Robotics\n\narXiv:2602.13977 (cs)\n\n[Submitted on 15 Feb 2026]\n\n# Title:WoVR: World Models as Reliable Simulators for Post-Training VLA Policies with RL\n\nAuthors: [Zhennan Jiang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Jiang,+Z), [Shangqing Zhou](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Zhou,+S), [Yutong Jiang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Jiang,+Y), [Zefang Huang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Huang,+Z), [Mingjie Wei](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Wei,+M), [Yuhui Chen](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Chen,+Y), [Tianxing Zhou](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Zhou,+T), [Zhen Guo](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Guo,+Z), [Hao Lin](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Lin,+H), [Quanlu Zhang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Zhang,+Q), [Yu Wang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Wang,+Y), [Haoran Li](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Li,+H), [Chao Yu](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Yu,+C), [Dongbin Zhao](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Zhao,+D)\n\n[View PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.13977) [HTML (experimental)](https://arxiv.org/html/2602.13977v1)\n\n&gt; Abstract:Reinforcement learning (RL) promises to unlock capabilities beyond imitation learning for Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, but its requirement for massive real-world interaction prevents direct deployment on physical robots. Recent work attempts to use learned world models as simulators for policy optimization, yet closed-loop imagined rollouts inevitably suffer from hallucination and long-horizon error accumulation. Such errors do not merely degrade visual fidelity; they corrupt the optimization signal, encouraging policies to exploit model inaccuracies rather than genuine task progress. We propose WoVR, a reliable world-model-based reinforcement learning framework for post-training VLA policies. Instead of assuming a faithful world model, WoVR explicitly regulates how RL interacts with imperfect imagined dynamics. It improves rollout stability through a controllable action-conditioned video world model, reshapes imagined interaction to reduce effective error depth via Keyframe-Initialized Rollouts, and maintains policy-simulator alignment through World Model-Policy co-evolution. Extensive experiments on LIBERO benchmarks and real-world robotic manipulation demonstrate that WoVR enables stable long-horizon imagined rollouts and effective policy optimization, improving average LIBERO success from 39.95% to 69.2% (+29.3 points) and real-robot success from 61.7% to 91.7% (+30.0 points). These results show that learned world models can serve as practical simulators for reinforcement learning when hallucination is explicitly controlled.\n\n[https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2602.13977](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2602.13977)\n\narXiv-issued DOI via DataCite\n\n| Comments: |\n| --- |\n| Subjects: | Robotics (cs.RO);   "url": "https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.12628",
  "markdown": "[2602.12628] Beyond Imitation: Reinforcement Learning-Based Sim-Real Co-Training for VLA Models\n\n--\n\n# Computer Science Robotics\n\narXiv:2602.12628 (cs)\n\n[Submitted on 13 Feb 2026 ([v1](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.12628v1)), last revised 6 Mar 2026 (this version, v3)]\n\n# Title:Beyond Imitation: Reinforcement Learning-Based Sim-Real Co-Training for VLA Models\n\nAuthors: [Liangzhi Shi](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Shi,+L), [Shuaihang Chen](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Chen,+S), [Feng Gao](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Gao,+F), [Yinuo Chen](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Chen,+Y), [Kang Chen](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Chen,+K), [Tonghe Zhang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Zhang,+T), [Hongzhi Zang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Zang,+H), [Weinan Zhang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Zhang,+W), [Chao Yu](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Yu,+C), [Yu Wang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Wang,+Y)\n\n[View PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.12628) [HTML (experimental)](https://arxiv.org/html/2602.12628v3)\n\n&gt; Abstract:Simulation offers a scalable and low-cost way to enrich vision-language-action (VLA) training, reducing reliance on expensive real-robot demonstrations. However, most sim-real co-training methods rely on supervised fine-tuning (SFT), which treats simulation as a static source of demonstrations and does not exploit large-scale closed-loop interaction. Consequently, real-world gains and generalization are often limited. In this paper, we propose an \\underline{\\textit{RL}}-based sim-real \\underline{\\textit{Co}}-training \\modify{(RL-Co)} framework that leverages interactive simulation while preserving real-world capabilities. Our method follows a generic two-stage design: we first warm-start the policy with SFT on a mixture of real and simulated demonstrations, then fine-tune it with reinforcement learning in simulation while adding an auxiliary supervised loss on real-world data to anchor the policy and mitigate catastrophic forgetting. We evaluate our framework on four real-world tabletop manipulation tasks using two representative VLA architectures, OpenVLA and $\\pi_{0.5}$, and observe consistent improvements over real-only fine-tuning and SFT-based co-training, including +24% real-world success on OpenVLA and +20% on $\\pi_{0.5}$. Beyond higher success rates, RL co-training yields stronger generalization to unseen task variations and substantially improved real-world data efficiency, providing a practical and scalable pathway for leveraging simulation to enhance real-robot deployment.\n\n[https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2602.12628](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2602.12628)\n\narXiv-issued DOI via DataCite\n\n| Subjects: |   "markdown": "[2506.15799] Steering Your Diffusion Policy with Latent Space Reinforcement Learning\n\n--\n\n# Computer Science Robotics\n\narXiv:2506.15799 (cs)\n\n[Submitted on 18 Jun 2025 ([v1](https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.15799v1)), last revised 25 Jun 2025 (this version, v2)]\n\n# Title:Steering Your Diffusion Policy with Latent Space Reinforcement Learning\n\nAuthors: [Andrew Wagenmaker](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Wagenmaker,+A), [Mitsuhiko Nakamoto](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Nakamoto,+M), [Yunchu Zhang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Zhang,+Y), [Seohong Park](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Park,+S), [Waleed Yagoub](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Yagoub,+W), [Anusha Nagabandi](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Nagabandi,+A), [Abhishek Gupta](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Gupta,+A), [Sergey Levine](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Levine,+S)\n\n[View PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2506.15799) [HTML (experimental)](https://arxiv.org/html/2506.15799v2)\n\n&gt; Abstract:Robotic control policies learned from human demonstrations have achieved impressive results in many real-world applications. However, in scenarios where initial performance is not satisfactory, as is often the case in novel open-world settings, such behavioral cloning (BC)-learned policies typically require collecting additional human demonstrations to further improve their behavior -- an expensive and time-consuming process. In contrast, reinforcement learning (RL) holds the promise of enabling autonomous online policy improvement, but often falls short of achieving this due to the large number of samples it typically requires. In this work we take steps towards enabling fast autonomous adaptation of BC-trained policies via efficient real-world RL. Focusing in particular on diffusion policies -- a state-of-the-art BC methodology -- we propose diffusion steering via reinforcement learning (DSRL): adapting the BC policy by running RL over its latent-noise space. We show that DSRL is highly sample efficient, requires only black-box access to the BC policy, and enables effective real-world autonomous policy improvement. Furthermore, DSRL avoids many of the challenges associated with finetuning diffusion policies, obviating the need to modify the weights of the base policy at all. We demonstrate DSRL on simulated benchmarks, real-world robotic tasks, and for adapting pretrained generalist policies, illustrating its sample efficiency and effective performance at real-world policy improvement.\n\n[https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2506.15799](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2506.15799)\n\narXiv-issued DOI via DataCite\n\n| Subjects: | Robotics (cs.RO); Machine Learning (cs.LG) |\n| --- | --- |\n| Cite as: | [arXiv:2506.15799](https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.15799) [cs.RO] |\n| (or [arXiv:2506.15799v2](https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.15799v2) [cs.RO] for this version) |\n\n##   "markdown": "[2509.25756] SAC Flow: Sample-Efficient Reinforcement Learning of Flow-Based Policies via Velocity-Reparameterized Sequential Modeling\n\n--\n\n# Computer Science Robotics\n\narXiv:2509.25756 (cs)\n\n[Submitted on 30 Sep 2025 ([v1](https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.25756v1)), last revised 14 Jan 2026 (this version, v3)]\n\n# Title:SAC Flow: Sample-Efficient Reinforcement Learning of Flow-Based Policies via Velocity-Reparameterized Sequential Modeling\n\nAuthors: [Yixian Zhang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Zhang,+Y), [Shu'ang Yu](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Yu,+S), [Tonghe Zhang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Zhang,+T), [Mo Guang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Guang,+M), [Haojia Hui](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Hui,+H), [Kaiwen Long](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Long,+K), [Yu Wang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Wang,+Y), [Chao Yu](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Yu,+C), [Wenbo Ding](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&amp;amp;query=Ding,+W)\n\n[View PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2509.25756) [HTML (experimental)](https://arxiv.org/html/2509.25756v3)\n\n&gt; Abstract:Training expressive flow-based policies with off-policy reinforcement learning is notoriously unstable due to gradient pathologies in the multi-step action sampling process. We trace this instability to a fundamental connection: the flow rollout is algebraically equivalent to a residual recurrent computation, making it susceptible to the same vanishing and exploding gradients as RNNs. To address this, we reparameterize the velocity network using principles from modern sequential models, introducing two stable architectures: Flow-G, which incorporates a gated velocity, and Flow-T, which utilizes a decoded velocity. We then develop a practical SAC-based algorithm, enabled by a noise-augmented rollout, that facilitates direct end-to-end training of these policies. Our approach supports both from-scratch and offline-to-online learning and achieves state-of-the-art performance on continuous control and robotic manipulation benchmarks, eliminating the need for common workarounds like policy distillation or surrogate objectives.\n\n[https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2509.25756](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2509.25756)\n\narXiv-issued DOI via DataCite\n\n| ---

RLinf 可以把它理解成一个**“面向基础模型后训练的 RL 系统层框架”**，而不是单一算法库。  
它的核心价值不只是“实现了 PPO/GRPO”，而是把**多组件 RL 工作流**（环境、生成、奖励、训练、同步）在分布式集群上以更高利用率跑起来。

## 一个体系化理解框架（建议你这样记）

- **第 1 层：问题层**  
  目标是解决 RL 训练里常见的系统痛点：组件异构、负载动态变化、GPU 空转、多节点部署复杂。
- **第 2 层：抽象层**  
  用 `Cluster`、`ComponentPlacement`、`Worker/WorkerGroup`、`Channel/Collective` 这些统一抽象，把“算法逻辑”与“资源调度/通信”解耦。
- **第 3 层：执行层**  
  支持 `collocated` / `disaggregated` / `hybrid` / `auto(dynamic scheduling)` 四类执行模式，按任务瓶颈调整资源分配。
- **第 4 层：算法层**  
  算法通过 registry 解耦（advantage/loss/reward 可插拔），支持 PPO、GRPO、DAPO、REINFORCE++、SAC、CrossQ、RLPD、Async PPO 等。
- **第 5 层：场景层**  
  同一套系统支持：推理 RL（数学）、具身 RL（仿真+真机）、Agentic（搜索/在线 coding RL）、world-model RL、sim-real 协同训练。
- **第 6 层：研究与工程闭环层**  
  论文/技术报告（RLinf、RLinf‑VLA、πRL、RLinf‑USER、WoVR、RL‑Co）不断把新方法落到同一基础设施里，形成“系统 + 方法”双轮迭代。

---

## RLinf 有什么功能（从“系统能力”看）

- **分布式训练编排**：Ray 集群管理、worker 生命周期管理、跨节点资源发现。  
- **灵活资源放置**：`cluster.component_placement` 可以把 actor/rollout/env/inference/reward 映射到不同 GPU/节点/硬件组。  
- **异构硬件支持**：不只 GPU，文档与代码都支持把机器人硬件当一等资源（如 Franka）。  
- **通信抽象**：`Channel`（队列化生产者-消费者）+ `Collective`（P2P/张量通信）做异步流水线。  
- **多后端并存**：训练侧 `FSDP` 或 `Megatron`；推理侧 `SGLang` / `vLLM` / `HF`。  
- **自动调度能力**：Auto placement、dynamic scheduling、online scaling（但有后端约束，见下文缺点）。  
- **可扩展机制**：模型/环境/算法通过枚举+registry+worker 接口扩展。  
- **训练工程能力**：日志、checkpoint、resume、评测、CI/e2e 配置较全（仓库下 `tests` 覆盖面比较广）。

---

## 可以用在什么场景

- **大规模 LLM 推理/数学 RL**：GRPO/DAPO/REINFORCE++ 等推理类后训练，强调吞吐与多节点编排。  
- **具身智能 RL**：VLA 模型在 ManiSkill、LIBERO、IsaacLab、MetaWorld、RoboCasa、RoboTwin、CALVIN 等。  
- **真实机器人在线学习**：RLinf‑USER 路线强调多机器人、异构机器人、云边协同与异步训练。  
- **Agentic 在线 RL**：如 coding online RL、Search‑R1/rStar2 这类“带工具调用和在线反馈”的训练闭环。  
- **世界模型 + RL**：如 WoVR/Wan/OpenSora 场景，把 world model 当可控模拟器做后训练。  

---

## 设计思想（你问的“为什么这么设计”）

### 1) M2Flow：宏观逻辑流 vs 微观执行流解耦
系统论文的关键词是 **Macro-to-Micro Flow Transformation**。核心是：  
先让用户写“逻辑流程”（rollout→reward→update），再由系统做时空分解、重组和调度优化。

### 2) Worker-first 的系统编程范式
不是先写“单机训练 loop”，而是先定义 Worker 角色和它们之间的数据流。  
入口脚本通常是“建 Cluster → 解析 Placement → 启 WorkerGroup → Runner 驱动”。

```38:75:examples/embodiment/train_embodied_agent.py
cluster = Cluster(cluster_cfg=cfg.cluster)
component_placement = HybridComponentPlacement(cfg, cluster)

# Create actor worker group
actor_placement = component_placement.get_strategy("actor")
...
runner = EmbodiedRunner(
    cfg=cfg,
    actor=actor_group,
    rollout=rollout_group,
    env=env_group,
)

runner.init_workers()
runner.run()
```

### 3) 配置驱动（Hydra/YAML）+ 强校验
`validate_cfg` 会按任务类型和后端做强约束，防止很多“跑到一半才炸”的配置问题。  
代价是学习曲线更陡。

### 4) 执行模式“按瓶颈选”而非“一刀切”
- collocated：简单，但容易内存竞争和长尾。  
- disaggregated：更灵活，但可能出现数据依赖导致空转。  
- hybrid：折中，强调细粒度 pipeline。  
- auto：动态迁移资源，进一步提升利用率。

---

## 代码架构（从实现角度）

高层职责分得比较清楚：

- `rlinf/scheduler`：集群、放置、worker、通信、动态调度。  
- `rlinf/runners`：训练主循环（reasoning/embodied/async/coding）。  
- `rlinf/workers`：actor/rollout/env/reward/inference 的具体执行体。  
- `rlinf/algorithms`：adv/loss/reward 注册与计算。  
- `rlinf/models` + `rlinf/envs`：模型/环境注册和加载。  
- `examples`：不同任务入口脚本与配置。

一个典型 reasoning 训练循环是“rollout→reward→(optional inference)→actor update”：

```391:418:rlinf/runners/reasoning_runner.py
rollout_handle: Handle = self.rollout.rollout(
    input_channel=self.dataloader_channel,
    output_channel=self.rollout_channel,
)

reward_handle: Handle = self.reward.compute_rewards(
    input_channel=self.rollout_channel,
    output_channel=self.reward_channel,
)

if self.recompute_logprobs:
    infer_handle: Handle = self.inference.run_inference(
        input_channel=self.reward_channel,
        output_channel=self.inference_channel,
        compute_ref_logprobs=self.compute_ref_logprobs,
    )
...
actor_handle: Handle = self.actor.run_training(
    input_channel=inference_channel,
)
```

`WorkerGroup` 在 launch 时把 rank/world-size/可见设备等环境注入 worker，再由 `Cluster.allocate()` 固定到目标节点：

```241:281:rlinf/scheduler/worker/worker_group.py
env_vars = {
    "GROUP_NAME": self._worker_group_name,
    "WORKER_NAME": worker_name,
    "MASTER_ADDR": master_addr,
    "WORLD_SIZE": str(self._world_size),
    "RANK": str(placement.rank),
    ...
    "VISIBLE_DEVICES": ",".join(placement.visible_accelerators),
    ...
}
worker = self._cluster.allocate(
    cls=self._worker_cls,
    worker_name=worker_name,
    node_rank=placement.cluster_node_rank,
    ...
)
```

---

## 这种实现的优点和缺点（客观权衡）

### 优点
- **系统灵活性高**：同一框架覆盖 reasoning/embodied/agentic/real-world。  
- **资源利用率导向**：placement + pipeline + dynamic scheduling 的组合拳。  
- **扩展性好**：算法 registry、模型/环境注册路径清晰。  
- **工程化成熟度较强**：examples、文档、unit/e2e 测试覆盖面都不错。  
- **适合“系统+算法联合优化”**：不仅能试算法，也能试调度策略与执行模式。

### 缺点
- **复杂度高**：要理解 Ray、placement、worker 通信、配置体系，门槛显著高于单机 RL 库。  
- **依赖较重**：尤其 Megatron/推理后端/多节点环境，对工程基础设施要求高。  
- **高级特性有约束**：例如 dynamic scheduler 在代码里明确只支持特定后端组合。  

```46:51:rlinf/scheduler/dynamic_scheduler/scheduler_worker.py
assert self.cfg.rollout.rollout_backend in ["sglang", "vllm"], (
    "only sglang and vllm are supported for dynamic scheduler"
)
assert self.cfg.actor.training_backend == "megatron", (
    "only megatron is supported for dynamic scheduler"
)
```

- **调参负担可能大**：异步/流水线场景还要兼顾 stale samples 与吞吐平衡。  
- **复现与宣称需分开看**：性能提升数据多来自官方实验，跨组织复现需要一致硬件/配置。  
- **文档演进快**：个别文档和脚本命名会有时序差异（快速迭代项目常见）。

---

## 一句话选型建议

- 如果你要做的是**大规模、跨场景、分布式 RL 后训练系统**（尤其涉及 VLA/LLM/Agent 混合），RLinf 非常值得。  
- 如果你只做**小规模单机算法验证**，RLinf 可能偏重，轻量库会更快上手。

---
